_description: Shared model registry for all AIXIA-workspace projects. Projects resolve model family → current_version at runtime from this file. Maintained by the bi-weekly model-release crawler (scheduled trigger). See ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md §9.5 for policy.
_last_manual_edit: 2026-07-20 — Out-of-band (non-crawler) addition of the `kimi` family (Moonshot AI, kimi-k3) at user request, to back the Hermes/AIXIA assistant, plus a new `agentic_assistant` routing default pointing at it. Verified via WebSearch (OpenRouter, VentureBeat, Tom's Hardware, MLQ) since Perplexity/Firecrawl remain network-blocked. JURISDICTION NOTE: Moonshot AI is China-hosted — deliberately EXCLUDED from privacy_mode_routing (Privacy Mode stays Claude-only). The next scheduled crawler run (2026-07-27) should re-verify this entry.
_bootstrap_note: This is the bootstrap state from 2026-04-20. Entries marked bootstrap_unverified: true are seeded from CLAUDE Code context and should be verified on the first crawler run. Pricing drifts; registry values are authoritative — always re-read at call-time.

claude_opus.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; no change. claude-opus-4-8 at $5/$25 per million remains current flagship. SWE-Bench Pro 69.2%, OSWorld-Verified 83.4%. Fast Mode at $10/$50 per million. Available on Claude API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, GitHub Copilot.

claude_fable.notes: 🟢 API RESTORED July 1, 2026 (was suspended June 12–July 1, 19 days total). Restoration: US government export-control directive lifted after Anthropic added: (1) a new cybersecurity classifier blocking the reported jailbreak technique in >99% of cases and rerouting flagged requests to Claude Opus 4.8, (2) wider safety margin, (3) accepted government conditions, (4) HackerOne disclosure channel. Suspension backstory: Amazon researchers found a method that prompted Fable 5 to surface software vulnerabilities and produce exploit code; US Commerce Dept issued directive June 12 citing national security; Anthropic executed global shutdown (could not verify nationality in real-time). Access metering post-restoration: Pro/Max/Team/select Enterprise get up to 50% of weekly limits on usage credits. Anthropic extended included access through July 12, then again through July 19, 2026. Pricing unchanged: $10/$50 per million tokens; prompt caching $1/M cache hits; batch API $5/$25 per million; full 1M context at flat rate. AIXIA PROJECTS: calls to claude-fable-5 should succeed again — remove any interim routing to claude_opus for Fable workloads.

claude_sonnet.notes: UPDATED Jul 13, 2026: claude-sonnet-4-6 → claude-sonnet-5 (released June 30, 2026). Context window 200K → 1M tokens with no long-context pricing premium. Up to 128K output tokens. Described by Anthropic as the most agentic Sonnet yet; new default on Free and Pro plans. PRICING: $2/$10 per million (launch rate through August 31, 2026); standard rate reverts to $3/$15 from September 1, 2026. Set pricing to current launch rate; audit downstream cost models before September 1 if budgeted at launch rate. Model ID: claude-sonnet-5. Available on Claude API, Bedrock, Vertex AI.

claude_haiku.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; no change. Pricing and version stable at $1/$5 per million.

deepseek_general.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; no pricing or version change. DeepSeek V4 Flash officially GA as of mid-July 2026 (preview period ended). 284B total / 13B active params (MoE), 1M context, Thinking+Non-Thinking dual mode. Cache hits at 1/10 standard rate ($0.028/M). Compatible with OpenAI ChatCompletions and Anthropic API formats. Chinese-server jurisdiction — disclose in ToS. Router fallback: deepseek → claude_haiku → claude_sonnet.

deepseek_reasoning.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; no change. Pricing confirmed $0.55/$2.19 (cache hits: $0.14/M input). DeepSeek R2 not released this cycle. R1 remains ~96% cheaper than OpenAI o1 for comparable tasks. Note: deepseek_v4_pro supports Thinking mode at 1M context if R1's 64K limit is too small.

deepseek_v4_pro.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; pricing confirmed at $0.435/$0.87 per million tokens (permanent rate; 75% discount made permanent May 22, 2026). V4 Pro officially GA as of mid-July 2026 (preview period ended). NEW (Jul 2026): DeepSeek introduced time-of-day peak pricing for V4 — peak hours (09:00–12:00 and 14:00–18:00 China time) billed at 2× the off-peak rate; off-peak rate = $0.435/$0.87 (registry values). At peak hours effective rate is $0.87/$1.74 — projects with latency-insensitive workloads should queue requests during off-peak windows. Cache hits: $0.003625/M input. 1.6T total / 49B active params (large MoE), 1M context, Thinking+Non-Thinking dual mode.

gemini_pro_multimodal.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; version and pricing unchanged. WATCH: Gemini 3.5 Pro targeting July 17, 2026 GA — missed June deadline (Sundar Pichai 'by end of June'), slipped to July. Currently in limited Vertex AI enterprise preview. Features: 2M token context, Deep Think reasoning mode, frontier multimodal. Pricing not yet published (estimates range widely; do not rely on third-party guesses). Do NOT update this entry until GA is confirmed. Tiered pricing for current 3.1 Pro: $2/$12 up to 200K tokens; $4/$18 above 200K. Gemini Pro paid-only (free tier removed April 1, 2026). YouTube URL ingest via file_data.file_uri.

gemini_flash.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; no change. 🚨 PRICING TRIPLED Jun 15: gemini-3.1-flash → gemini-3.5-flash (GA May 19, 2026). $0.50→$1.50 input, $3.00→$9.00 output per million tokens (200% increase). All AIXIA projects routing bulk text or coding tasks through gemini_flash must re-audit cost models. Context: 1,048,576 tokens. Cached input: $0.15/M (90% discount). Non-global regions: $1.65/$9.90. Batch API: 50% discount. Budget alternative: gemini-3.1-flash-lite still available at lower pricing.

openai_gpt.notes: UPDATED Jul 13, 2026: gpt-5.5 → gpt-5.6-sol (GA July 9, 2026, after limited preview starting June 26). GPT-5.6 family launched with three tiers: Sol (flagship, gpt-5.6-sol, $5/$30, 1.05M context, max+ultra effort modes, parallel sub-model dispatch); Terra (gpt-5.6-terra, $2.50/$15, 1.05M context); Luna (gpt-5.6-luna, $1/$6, fastest/cheapest). Sol pricing unchanged from gpt-5.5 ($5/$30) but context bumped 1M→1.05M. Long-context surcharge: >272K input tokens billed at $10 input/$45 output for full request. Cache pricing: $0.50/M cache hits, $6.25/M cache writes, 30-minute minimum cache life (improvement over gpt-5.5). New: multi-agent mode (beta) lets Sol dispatch concurrent subagents in a single request. API model IDs: gpt-5.6-sol, gpt-5.6-terra, gpt-5.6-luna (gpt-5.6 aliases to Sol). Self-serve: no waitlist, no plan gating. GPT-5.5 Instant remains ChatGPT default. GPT-4.5 removed from ChatGPT June 26. Fine-tuning API deprecated — any AIXIA project on fine-tuned GPT must migrate. Not currently used by AIXIA projects.

openai_reasoning.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; no change. o4-mini outperforms o3-mini at ~50% lower cost. o3 also available ($2.00/M input, 200K context). IMPORTANT: reasoning tokens billed at output rate — effective cost 5–20× listed rate. No o5 release confirmed this cycle. Not currently used by AIXIA projects.

mistral_large.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; no change. Mistral Large 3 2512: 262K context, EU-hosted option for data-residency-sensitive tiers. No new Large model this cycle. Mistral released Leanstral 1.5 (Lean 4 formal proof engineering, retiring Sep 30 2026) and OCR 4 (mistral-ocr-4-0, $4/1K pages) this period — neither affects base LM routing.

mistral_small.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; no change. Mistral Small 4 (mistral-small-2603): MoE 119B total / 6B active params, 256K context, native multimodal. Cheapest EU-residency multimodal option at $0.15/$0.60.

mistral_medium.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; no change. Mistral Medium 3.5 (April 29, 2026): dense 128B open-weight; unifies instruction-following, reasoning, and coding. 256K context, EU data residency. Output cost ($7.50/M) is 5× mistral_large — only worth it when unified reasoning+coding at long context is required.

mistral_reasoning.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; no change. magistral-medium-2506: $2.00/$5.00 per million tokens, 41K context. Budget option: magistral-small-2506 at $0.50/$1.50, 40K context. EU data residency via La Plateforme. Compact context (41K) suits focused reasoning — switch to mistral_medium (256K) when long context is also needed.

llama.notes: Verified Jul 13, 2026; no new open-weight Llama release this cycle. Llama 4 Maverick remains current open-weight offering: 17B active params, 128 experts (400B total), 1M context, natively multimodal MoE. Pricing reflects third-party providers (Together AI, Fireworks, Groq, AWS Bedrock). Also: Llama 4 Scout ($0.15/$0.60, 10M context) for extreme-context tasks; Llama 4 Behemoth (288B active) still not GA. Meta focus has shifted toward Muse Spark (proprietary, not open-weight) — not tracked here as it is not available via standard open-weight routes relevant to AIXIA projects.

grok.notes: UPDATED Jul 13, 2026: grok-4.3 → grok-4.5 (released July 8, 2026). 🚨 TWO BREAKING CHANGES: (1) Context window HALVED: 1M → 500K tokens — any AIXIA project using Grok for context >500K must migrate to gemini_pro_multimodal (2M). (2) Output pricing +140%: $2.50 → $6.00/M output (well above 20% threshold); input +60%: $1.25 → $2.00/M. Architecture: built on V9 foundation (1.5T params), pitched as 'Opus-class but faster and more token-efficient.' Trained alongside Cursor on real coding-agent data — primary use case is engineering and agentic work. Configurable reasoning (low/medium/high), function calling, web search, X search, code execution, prompt caching supported. Available via xAI console (grok-4.5), Cursor (all plans), and Grok Build (default model). EU availability not confirmed as of launch. Knowledge cutoff: February 1, 2026. NOT yet available in EU. Previous grok-4.3 ($1.25/$2.50, 1M context) no longer the current version.

kimi.notes: ADDED Jul 20, 2026 (manual, out-of-band — not a crawler run). Moonshot AI Kimi K3, API GA July 16, 2026 at https://api.moonshot.ai/v1, model ID kimi-k3. 2.8T total params open-weight MoE (16 of 896 experts active per request), 1,048,576-token context, multimodal reasoning. Pricing $3/$15 per million input/output; cache-hit input $0.30/M; built-in web search billed +$0.015/call. Open weights slated for release ~July 27, 2026 under a Modified MIT license (self-hosting possible once out). Benchmarks: reported to edge out Claude Fable 5 in Frontend Code Arena (Tom's Hardware) — positioned as a frontier agentic/coding model, which is why it backs the Hermes/AIXIA autonomous assistant. Promo: up to 30% API-credit voucher rebate on top-ups ≥$1,000 through Aug 12, 2026. 🚨 JURISDICTION: Moonshot AI is China-hosted — treat like deepseek (disclose in ToS; do NOT use for data-residency-sensitive or Privacy Mode workloads). Deliberately EXCLUDED from privacy_mode_routing. Verified via WebSearch (OpenRouter, VentureBeat, MLQ, Tom's Hardware); re-verify at next crawler run (2026-07-27).

routing_defaults.description: Canonical default routing for common task categories. Projects override as needed in their local CLAUDE.md.

privacy_mode_routing.description: When a project/tier enables Privacy Mode, all calls route through Claude only (no DeepSeek, no third-party-jurisdiction routes). Studio-tier Echoform users default to Privacy Mode.
